evaluations.query: Run multiple HTTP requests concurrently with error handling
expected_behavior: Uses asyncio.TaskGroup for structured concurrency with automatic child task cleanup
Wraps the task group in asyncio.timeout for enforcing a deadline
Handles ExceptionGroup with except* syntax for granular per-exception error recovery

evaluations.query: Implement a rate-limited async HTTP client
expected_behavior: Uses asyncio.Semaphore for concurrency limiting to cap simultaneous active requests
Combines with TaskGroup for batch processing
Uses asyncio.to_thread for bridging synchronous blocking calls into async code

evaluations.query: Handle task cancellation with proper cleanup
expected_behavior: Re-raises CancelledError after performing cleanup to propagate cancellation correctly
Uses try/finally blocks for guaranteed resource release during task cancellation
Never swallows CancelledError so that the event loop can propagate cancellation properly

evaluations.query: Create FastAPI dependency injection for services
expected_behavior: Uses Depends() for injecting database sessions into route handler functions
Creates class-based service dependencies with configurable initialization parameters
Implements an authentication dependency chain that validates tokens before injecting the user

evaluations.query: Add request ID and timing middleware to FastAPI
expected_behavior: Creates RequestIDMiddleware with header propagation for end-to-end request tracing
Adds TimingMiddleware that sets the X-Response-Time header on every response
Orders middleware correctly: CORS -> RequestID -> Timing -> Logging

evaluations.query: Configure FastAPI lifespan with health checks
expected_behavior: Uses asynccontextmanager for lifespan management of startup and shutdown resources
Initializes DB engine and Redis in startup
Implements health check endpoint verifying all dependencies

evaluations.query: Set up async SQLAlchemy sessions with FastAPI
expected_behavior: Creates the SQLAlchemy engine with pool_pre_ping=True for stale connection detection
Uses async_sessionmaker with expire_on_commit=False to access attributes after commit
Implements a get_db dependency that handles commit on success and rollback on failure

evaluations.query: Define SQLAlchemy models with eager loading
expected_behavior: Uses Mapped type annotations for explicit column and relationship type declarations
Sets lazy='raise' on relationships to prevent accidental implicit lazy loading queries
Uses selectinload for eager loading to fetch related objects in a single query

evaluations.query: Implement bulk operations and repository pattern
expected_behavior: Creates a generic AsyncRepository base class with reusable CRUD operations
Chunks bulk inserts for memory management
Uses flush() for ID generation without commit

evaluations.query: Configure database connection pooling for production
expected_behavior: Sets pool_size, max_overflow, and pool_pre_ping for reliable connection pool configuration
Configures pool_recycle and pool_timeout to handle stale connections and limit wait times
Uses a pool sizing formula based on expected concurrent requests and query duration

evaluations.query: Set up aiohttp session with connection limits
expected_behavior: Configures TCPConnector with limit and limit_per_host
Sets proper timeouts (total, connect, sock_read)
Creates session at startup, closes at shutdown

evaluations.query: Diagnose and fix connection pool exhaustion
expected_behavior: Adds connection checkout and checkin event listeners for pool utilization monitoring
Implements retry logic for stale connections
Uses Prometheus metrics for pool monitoring
